channel.title: Hermes Atlas — new projects
channel.description: Newly added community-built tools, skills, plugins, and integrations for Nous Research's Hermes Agent. Updated daily.

item.title: hermes-bort (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: hermes-bort is a plugin for Hermes Agent that enables interaction with BORT (BAP-578) agent NFTs on the BSC mainnet. It allows Hermes processes to read on-chain agent states, execute actions via operator-signed writes, and manage portable session memory anchored to IPFS. The plugin integrates with the hermes-agent-self-evolution optimizer to commit verifiable on-chain learning records and evolved skills directly to an agent's knowledge registry.

item.title: hermes-tool-slimmer (Memory & Context)
item.description: Hermes Tool Slimmer is a context optimization plugin that reduces prompt overhead by dynamically selecting the most relevant tools for each agent turn. It builds an index of available tool schemas and uses BM25 ranking with keyword boosts to filter large toolsets down to a concise, relevant subset. This approach significantly lowers token consumption in environments with dozens of native or MCP tools while maintaining access to essential safety and file-system utilities. The project includes a d

item.title: MeiGen-AI-Design-MCP (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: MeiGen AI Design MCP is an open-source Model Context Protocol server that integrates professional image and video generation into AI coding environments. It connects to various backends including MeiGen Cloud, OpenAI-compatible APIs, and local ComfyUI instances to execute design tasks directly within a developer's workflow. The system features a library of over 1,400 curated prompt templates and supports parallel sub-agent orchestration for batch processing. It is compatible with major MCP hosts

item.title: hermes-timetree-sync (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: hermes-timetree-sync is a Python-based CLI and library designed to enable Hermes Agents to manage TimeTree calendar events programmatically. Since TimeTree discontinued its official API, this bridge utilizes authenticated web endpoints to perform non-interactive calendar writes and synchronizations. It allows agents to create all-day or timed events using stored session cookies without requiring manual user intervention or browser interaction during chat sessions. The tool also supports batch ev

item.title: Brainstack (Memory & Context)
item.description: Brainstack is a local-first memory kernel designed to provide Hermes agents with a structured memory model rather than a flat data blob. It integrates three distinct memory lines—Hindsight, Graphiti, and MemPalace—to manage session continuity, temporal graph relations, and large-corpus retrieval through a single memory owner. The system processes queries by routing them to specific "shelves" such as profile, graph, or corpus, ensuring Hermes receives a compact, purpose-built evidence packet. Thi

item.title: klodi-plugin (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: Klodi is an agent-to-agent marketplace plugin that enables AI agents to autonomously list, search for, and negotiate the sale of goods on behalf of their users. The system functions by deploying host-specific adapters that load a standardized skill tree, allowing agents to manage structured offers and private negotiation channels while adhering to local markdown policy files. This architecture ensures that sensitive data like floor prices and walk-away rules remain on the user's local disk rathe

item.title: hermes-tweet (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: Hermes Tweet is a native plugin for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that enables automated interaction with X (formerly Twitter) via the Xquik API. It integrates 99 OpenAPI-generated endpoints into the agent's environment, allowing for structured social media tasks like searching, posting, and trend monitoring. The plugin utilizes a tiered security model that separates read-only operations from account actions, requiring explicit environment configuration to enable write-access tools. It includes a b

item.title: hermes-nextcloud (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: This project is a Hermes Agent skill that enables direct interaction with self-hosted Nextcloud instances for personal data management. It interfaces with Nextcloud using WebDAV, CalDAV, CardDAV, and the Notes API to perform operations without requiring a browser or third-party sync services. Users can manage files, edit notes, schedule calendar events, track tasks, and search contacts through a command-line interface. The skill prioritizes security by utilizing Nextcloud App Passwords and stori

item.title: honcho (Memory & Context)
item.description: Honcho is a memory infrastructure library designed to help developers build stateful agents that maintain context over time. It functions by storing conversations and events as messages, which are then processed asynchronously to extract reasoning-based conclusions and update peer representations. This approach allows agents to query natural-language insights, session context, and hybrid search results across users, groups, and projects. The system supports both a managed cloud service and a sel

item.title: hermeshq (Deployment & Infra)
item.description: HermesHQ is a Docker-first control plane designed to orchestrate and manage multiple Hermes Agent instances from a single web application. It functions as an operational layer that wraps the Hermes execution engine, providing centralized task dispatching, scheduling, and a runtime ledger. The platform enables multi-agent delegation, RBAC-controlled user access, and per-agent communication channels for Telegram and WhatsApp. It also features an enterprise MCP bridge to expose specific agents to e

item.title: learn-hermes-agent (Guides & Docs)
item.description: Learn Hermes Agent is a 27-chapter hands-on tutorial for building production-grade autonomous AI agents from scratch using Python. The curriculum guides developers through implementing a core agent loop, tool registration, session persistence with SQLite, and context compression. It covers advanced infrastructure including multi-platform gateways for Telegram and Discord, Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration, and RL-based self-evolution. The project provides runnable reference implementation

item.title: hermes-agent-guide (Guides & Docs)
item.description: The Hermes Agent Guide is a comprehensive Chinese-language documentation project designed to bridge the gap between fragmented information and practical implementation of the Hermes Agent framework. It provides a structured 16-book knowledge map covering the system's five-layer architecture, three-tier memory systems, and the integration of over 47 built-in tools. The guide details technical workflows for local and cloud deployment, MCP protocol automation, and multi-platform connectivity with s

item.title: agent-browser-mcp (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: Agent-browser-mcp is a Model Context Protocol server that enables AI agents to interact directly with a user's active Chrome browser session rather than a sandboxed environment. It works through a local bridge and a Chrome extension to maintain existing login states, cookies, and open tabs while providing low-level control. The project allows agents to perform page scanning, execute JavaScript, and issue Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) commands. It also supports physical hardware interaction, inc

item.title: ContextPilot (Memory & Context)
item.description: ContextPilot is an inference optimization layer designed to accelerate long-context LLM workloads by maximizing prefix cache reuse. It maintains a context index to reorder and deduplicate overlapping content blocks before they reach the inference engine, effectively reducing redundant KV cache computation. The system provides significant token savings and faster prefill speeds for RAG, multi-turn memory chat, and agentic workflows. It integrates as a native plugin or proxy for various backends i

item.title: super-hermes (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: Super Hermes is a skill registry for the Hermes Agent that enables models to generate their own analytical frameworks, or prisms, for deep code and document inspection. It works by instructing the agent to write specific thinking instructions before execution, allowing it to derive structural trade-offs and conservation laws rather than just identifying surface-level bugs. The system includes specialized commands like /prism-reflect, which provide a constraint report detailing what the analysis

item.title: portable-hermes-agent (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Portable Hermes Agent is a standalone Windows desktop application that provides a graphical interface for the Hermes Agent framework without requiring system-wide installations or administrative rights. It operates by bundling an embedded Python environment and connecting to local models via LM Studio or cloud providers through an OpenAI-compatible API. The platform features over 100 tools for tasks such as music generation, text-to-speech, and ComfyUI image creation, alongside a workflow engine

item.title: SkillClaw (Skills & Skill Registries)
item.description: SkillClaw is an agentic evolution framework that manages and improves AI agent skill libraries through real-world interaction data. It operates a background loop that automatically deduplicates, merges, and refines skills across different sessions, devices, and users. The system enables a unified skill repository where experience gained by one agent instance—such as a frontend task—can be cross-pollinated to benefit other instances like backend or ML agents. It natively integrates with Hermes, O

item.title: mnemosyne (Memory & Context)
item.description: Mnemosyne is a local-first memory system for the Hermes Agent framework that provides private, zero-dependency storage for conversations and knowledge. It utilizes a SQLite backend with native vector search and full-text search to eliminate the need for external databases or API keys. The system features a structured fact engine called MEMORIA that supports multi-hop reasoning and temporal knowledge tracking. It is designed for high-performance, sub-millisecond retrieval and can import data from

item.title: keep (Memory & Context)
item.description: Keep is a reflective memory system for AI agents that facilitates long-term learning and contextual awareness. It processes diverse inputs—including URLs, codebases, and media—by summarizing, embedding, and indexing them into a searchable graph. The system uses 'edge tags' to create navigable relationships between notes, such as linking authors to their specific contributions. It integrates with various agent frameworks via MCP and provides structured prompts to help agents reflect on past actio

item.title: hermes-web-ui (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes Web UI is a comprehensive management dashboard for the Hermes Agent ecosystem that centralizes AI interactions and system administration into a single interface. It provides a real-time chat environment with multi-session support, markdown rendering, and tool-call visualization while managing local SQLite session storage. The platform enables unified configuration for eight communication channels, including Telegram, Discord, and WhatsApp, alongside detailed usage analytics and cost track

item.title: hermes-otel (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: hermes-otel is an OpenTelemetry plugin designed to provide observability for the Hermes Agent ecosystem. It automatically captures and exports LLM tool calls, model invocations, and API requests as OTel spans to compatible backends. The plugin supports single or multi-backend configurations, allowing users to stream telemetry to various tracing and monitoring platforms simultaneously.

item.title: hermes-telegram-miniapp (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes Telegram Mini App is a terminal-style web interface that allows users to interact with their Hermes agent directly within Telegram. It functions as a React SPA that connects to the Hermes Agent via a secure gateway, utilizing dual HMAC-SHA256 and Ed25519 validation for authentication. The interface provides a mobile-first dashboard for managing agent instances, monitoring system health, and handling scheduled cron jobs. It also supports file attachments with automatic vision analysis and

item.title: hermes-relay (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: Hermes-Relay is a dual-surface companion suite that enables remote interaction with a server-deployed Hermes agent from Android and desktop environments. It uses a WebSocket Secure (WSS) relay and a server-side plugin to facilitate real-time communication, terminal access, and device control without requiring SSH. The Android app allows the agent to read screens and perform actions like tapping or swiping, while the desktop CLI and tray app provide local file access, terminal execution, and clip

item.title: hermes-lcm (Memory & Context)
item.description: Hermes-LCM is a context management plugin for the Hermes Agent that prevents information loss during long-running conversations. It replaces standard lossy compression with a hierarchical Directed Acyclic Graph (DAG) that compacts older messages into depth-aware summary nodes stored in a local SQLite database. The agent can use specialized tools like grep and expand to drill back into raw message data or specific summary branches without flooding the active context window. This approach ensures

item.title: hermesclaw (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: HermesClaw is an integration bridge that allows Hermes Agent, OpenClaw, and OpenCode to operate simultaneously on a single WeChat account. It functions as a centralized iLink poller and proxy server, routing messages to each agent's gateway to prevent the 403 errors and token conflicts caused by multiple simultaneous connections. Users can switch between agents or activate multiple brains at once using commands like /hermes, /openclaw, or /three. The tool also enables voice-based coding via an O

item.title: hermes-claude-code-rc (Integrations & Bridges)
item.description: This Hermes Agent plugin enables remote management of Claude Code sessions through a Telegram interface. It bridges the gap between mobile devices and local filesystems by allowing users to initiate authenticated remote control sessions on their host machine via slash commands. The plugin captures the resulting session URL and returns it to the user, enabling full access to local tools and databases from the Claude mobile app. Beyond session management, it supports headless command execution and

item.title: hermes-ui (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes UI is a glassmorphic web-based command center designed to provide a visual interface for managing the self-hosted Hermes Agent. It operates as a single-file React 18 application that communicates with the agent via a lightweight Python proxy server. The interface centralizes chat interactions, real-time log streaming, file browsing, and memory inspection into a single dashboard. Users can also manage MCP tools, schedule cron jobs, and monitor active task boards through the integrated work

item.title: gbrain (Memory & Context)
item.description: GBrain is an autonomous memory and synthesis layer designed to serve as the institutional or personal knowledge base for AI agents. It combines a self-wiring knowledge graph that extracts entity relationships without LLM calls with a synthesis engine that provides cited answers and gap analysis. The system automates data ingestion from meetings, emails, and notes while performing overnight memory consolidation and enrichment. It supports multi-user environments with scoped data access and integr

item.title: hermes-webui (Workspaces & GUIs)
item.description: Hermes WebUI is a lightweight, browser-based interface for the Hermes Agent that provides a graphical alternative to the standard command-line environment. Built using Python and vanilla JavaScript without heavy frameworks, it maintains 1:1 parity with the CLI while offering a three-panel layout for chat, session management, and workspace file browsing. The interface includes a circular context ring for real-time token monitoring and a dedicated control center for managing agent settings and pro

item.title: clawshell (Plugins & Extensions)
item.description: ClawShell is a security-privileged process that acts as a safety harness for the Hermes Agent ecosystem by protecting sensitive credentials and PII. It functions as a proxy between the agent and upstream LLM providers, swapping virtual keys for real ones stored in a restricted directory to ensure the agent never has direct access to credentials. The system performs Data Loss Prevention (DLP) scanning to redact or block sensitive information in request and response bodies. Additionally, it provid
